headline: James answered with concrete constraints and uncertainty—this is a low-signal session on engagement/clarity, not a settled promotion recovery story.
summary_bullets: James’ engagement drop is tied to the promotion decision: “After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.”
He is using mentoring as a lifeline for usefulness, but he still doubts recognition: “I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.”
His next-step commitment is conditional on authority and precise criteria: “I can own bigger direction, but I need real authority to do it.”
understanding_paragraph: James feels less connected because he’s unclear on what must change after the promotion decision. He’s staying focused on controllable delivery, which keeps him moving but doesn’t restore confidence in how his work will be recognized. He wants a precisely defined path and real authority before he commits to bigger ownership.

axes.meaning: Weak signal but actionable: wellbeing is strained by post-decision uncertainty and reduced ritual presence.
Defining signal: James is disengaging from team rituals and is not leaning in until expectations and recognition are clarified.
Real pattern: he lacks clear criteria for what needs to change and what work will be recognized.
Meaningful constraint: he can grow only with authority and a defined path, not reassurance or vague direction.

brutal_truth_employee: James links his dip directly to the promotion outcome: “After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.” He’s not asking for comfort—“I want clearer criteria, not reassurance”—and he won’t commit to bigger scope without “real authority to do it.”
brutal_truth_manager: James gave you the exact decision-to-clarity failure chain: “After the promotion decision… not clear on what really needed to change.” The moment to lock this down was when he said “I want clearer criteria, not reassurance”—you should have extracted the specific criteria and the specific authority he needs, then turned it into a concrete decision and scope for him.

next_actions.action: Schedule a 30-minute criteria-defining meeting with James and name the exact promotion-success criteria he lacks, in writing.
Confirm James’ mentoring program charter: define whether it builds his next-level authority, and remove any mentoring work that doesn’t map to recognition criteria.
Ask James to propose a one-page “precise path” with scope, decision rights, and the first deliverable he can own end-to-end.

watch_for: Before next 1:1: if James still says “not clear on what really needed to change,” treat it as unresolved decision fallout, not a temporary dip.
Within two weeks: if mentoring remains “useful” but he still says “not sure the work… gets recognized,” you have an authority/criteria mismatch to fix.
